project.name: Andres Arreola Portfolio
project.description: Mechanical engineering portfolio. Hydropower specialist, 2026 DOE Hydropower Collegiate Competition national champion.
project.northStar: The Field Engineer's Notebook — authority through specificity, documentation that earns trust through exactness.

text-ink.role: Primary text, near-black with cool lean. INVARIANT — never swapped in dark mode on amber backgrounds.

accent.role: Measurement Amber. Fills: buttons, featured border, award badge. Never body text.

accent-text.role: Amber-for-text. ~6.5:1 on white. All amber text uses this level.

tag-bg.role: Light amber chip/badge surface. Pairs exclusively with accent-text.

colorMeta.rule: Two-level amber: accent (L=0.65) for fills/borders; accent-text (L=0.40) for foreground text. Never cross levels.

contrastAA.text-ink on accent: ~9.2:1 PASS (use text-ink, not text-muted or any var that changes in dark mode)

display.usage: Hero name only. One element per page.

label.usage: Tags, metadata, nav links, button text. Floor: never below 0.875rem on any viewport.

typographyMeta.rule: Barlow Condensed for h1/h2/h3 and nav logo only. All other text uses the system stack.
typographyMeta.scaleContrast: Display min (3.5rem) / Title (1.2rem) >= 2.9x. Each hierarchy step unmistakable at a glance.

card-rest.usage: Always present on cards. Two-layer: near sharp + far soft.

card-hover.usage: Card hover state. Pairs with translateY(-3px). Both must change simultaneously.

nav-scrolled.usage: Nav when hero has scrolled out of viewport. Applied via .scrolled class by IntersectionObserver.

btn-primary-hover.usage: Amber glow under primary CTA on hover. Disappears at rest.

keyframes.rise: from { opacity: 0; transform: translateY(20px); } to { opacity: 1; transform: none; }
keyframes.card-in: from { opacity: 0; transform: translateY(14px); } to { opacity: 1; transform: none; }

heroChoreography.strategy: Pure CSS @keyframes, fires on page load. No JS gating. animation-fill-mode: both keeps elements at 'from' state during delay.

cardEntrance.strategy: IntersectionObserver with progressive enhancement. .js class gates the opacity:0 initial state. Inline script in <head> adds .js synchronously before body renders.
cardEntrance.stagger: var(--stagger, 0) * 60ms via CSS custom property on each card element

mobileNav.strategy: opacity + visibility + pointer-events. transition: visibility 0s [delay] pattern for smooth close.

motion.rule: rise fires once on page load for the hero. No scroll-triggered fade-rise on every section. Card entrance is the only scroll-reveal in the system.

components.name: button-primary
components.description: Primary CTA. Amber fill, near-black text. The single most important action on any section.

components.name: button-outline
components.description: Secondary CTA. Transparent fill, amber border and text. Paired with button-primary in hero actions.
components.css:
.ds-btn-outline { background: transparent; color: oklch(0.40 0.12 65); border-color: oklch(0.65 0.13 65); }
.ds-btn-outline:hover { background: oklch(0.96 0.02 65); }

components.name: link-pill-primary
components.description: Filled amber pill for the primary project action per card (Live Demo, DOE Article).
components.html: Live Demo →

components.name: link-pill-outline
components.description: Outline pill for secondary project links (GitHub, article references). Groups with link-pill-primary in .card-links flex container.
components.css:
.ds-pill-outline { background: transparent; color: oklch(0.40 0.12 65); border-color: oklch(0.65 0.13 65); }
.ds-pill-outline:hover { background: oklch(0.96 0.02 65); }

components.name: card-default
components.description: Project card. Two-layer ambient shadow at rest, rises on hover. Stagger delay via --stagger CSS custom property.
components.html:
Category · Type 2025

Project Title

Card description paragraph.

Live Demo → GitHub →

components.name: card-featured
components.description: Hero project card. Full grid width, amber border, warm diagonal gradient. One per page.
components.html:
🏆 1st Place Overall

Featured Project
components.css:
.ds-card-featured { grid-column: 1 / -1; border-color: oklch(0.65 0.13 65); background: linear-gradient(135deg, oklch(0.96 0.02 65) 0%, oklch(1 0 0) 60%); }
.ds-card-award { font-size: 0.82rem; font-weight: 600; color: oklch(0.40 0.12 65); border: 1px solid color-mix(in srgb, oklch(0.65 0.13 65) 30%, transparent); background: color-mix(in srgb, oklch(0.65 0.13 65) 8%, transparent); border-radius: 6px; padding: 0.4rem 0.75rem; display: inline-block; margin-bottom: 0.75rem; }

components.name: tag-chip
components.description: Technology stack chip. Low visual weight. Used in card-tags lists to list tools without asserting hierarchy.
components.css:
.ds-tag-list { list-style: none; padding: 0; margin: 1rem 0 0; display: flex; flex-wrap: wrap; gap: 0.4rem; }
.ds-tag-list li { font-size: 0.8rem; color: oklch(0.47 0.015 260); border: 1px solid oklch(0.88 0.005 255); border-radius: 999px; padding: 0.2rem 0.6rem; }

components.name: hero-win
components.description: Competition result callout in the hero. Appears once. Amber-tinted surface with muted context line.
components.html: 1st Place Overall · 1st Place Build & Test 2026 DOE Hydropower Collegiate Competition · National

components.name: nav
components.description: Sticky navigation bar. Translucent at top, gains shadow when hero scrolls past (via .scrolled class from IntersectionObserver).
components.html:
Andres Arreola
  • About
  • Projects

narrative.northStar: The Field Engineer's Notebook — authority through specificity, documentation that earns trust through exactness.
narrative.accentCharacter: Measurement Amber: signal indicators, pressure gauges, test bench warning lights. A deliberate mark on what matters.
narrative.elevationPhilosophy: Flat by default. Shadows respond to state, not applied as decoration. One ambient exception: the card resting shadow, which always distinguishes cards from their background.
narrative.componentFeel: Designed like instruments, not decorations. Precise and legible. The card is a spec sheet; the button is a control.
antiPatterns: Warm-neutral body background (cream/sand/parchment). Background is pure white; warmth is carried by the amber accent.
Gradient text. Weight and scale carry emphasis; a single solid color always.
Tiny uppercase tracked eyebrows above every section.
scroll-triggered fade-rise on every section. The rise keyframe fires once: hero on page load.
A second accent color. The amber is singular; rarity is what makes it signal.
Using var(--c-text) for text on amber fills. Always use the hard-coded near-black invariant.
